session_title: Harnessing the Power of the Immune System: Turning Cold Tumors Hot
talk_title: Mechanisms of Myeloid-Mediated Resistance to Anti-PD-1 Therapy

biomarkers.name: gamma interferon gene signature
biomarkers.finding: Efficacy of single agent anti-PD-1 is enriched in patients with activated T cell gene signature (gamma interferon-induced genes) at baseline.

biomarkers.name: PKC delta hypomorphic allele
biomarkers.finding: Correlated with increased T cell infiltrate in the tumor microenvironment.

models.name: gnotobiotic mice
models.genotype_or_key_features: germ-free mice receiving human FMT from responders vs non-responders

MOA_summary: The speaker proposes that 'cold' tumors are dominated by suppressive M2-like macrophages that prevent T cell function. He identifies PKC delta and GPNMB as key negative regulators of the antitumor response; loss of these targets shifts macrophage phenotype from suppressive to inflammatory, thereby enhancing spontaneous tumor control and anti-PD-1 efficacy.
key_findings: FMT from responder patients into germ-free mice restored anti-PD-L1 efficacy, a phenotype retained generationally in offspring.
Single-cell RNA-seq of FMT mice showed that responder microbiota induced an inflammatory macrophage gene signature and flipped granulocytes away from an MDSC-like phenotype.
Conditional deletion of PKC delta in hematopoietic cells led to spontaneous tumor control and markedly improved anti-PD-1 efficacy.
GPNMB is dominantly expressed in SPP1+ macrophages; its knockout resulted in substantial spontaneous tumor control in B16-SIY and orthotopic breast cancer models.
GPNMB-mediated suppression is driven by tissue-resident macrophages rather than bone marrow-derived cells, as Lysozyme M-Cre deletion showed a phenotype while Vav1-Cre did not.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: STING agonists have limited efficacy in cold tumors because they require the presence of CDC1 cells to function.
open_questions: Identification of the relevant receptor for GPNMB to enable pharmacological blockade.
Whether the GPNMB pathway is generalizable to other cold tumors like pancreatic or colon cancer.

session_title: Harnessing the Power of the Immune System: Turning Cold Tumors Hot
talk_title: Genetic Screens and Heterotypic Clusters for Overcoming Immunotherapy Resistance

biomarkers.name: T cell-tumor heterotypic clusters
biomarkers.finding: T cells in clusters are highly enriched for tumor antigen specificity, clonal expansion, and exhibit a 9-fold increase in melanoma killing capacity compared to singlets.

models.name: Mart1-expressing melanoma cells
models.genotype_or_key_features: co-cultured with Mart1-specific TCR T cells

MOA_summary: The speaker used genome-wide CRISPR screens to identify TRAF2 and RNF31 as survival-arm TNF pathway components that, when inactivated, sensitize tumor cells to T cell killing. Additionally, he developed a pipeline to isolate T cell-tumor heterotypic clusters, which are enriched for tumor-reactive, clonally expanded T cells with superior killing capacity.
key_findings: Inactivation of TRAF2 or RNF31 tips the balance from pro-survival to pro-death, leading to tumor regression in immune-proficient mouse models.
DEP5 knockout in primary T cells boosts global translation and proliferation, leading to significant tumor control in adoptive transfer models.
CD8 T cell clusters comprise ~1.4% of all cells in melanoma lymph node metastases, with ~12% of total CD8 T cells residing in clusters.
T cells isolated from clusters show a 9-fold increase in melanoma killing capacity in vitro across 11 patients.
MGT2 was identified as a top hit in proximity labeling and cluster screens; its knockout in T cells enhances tumor killing in vitro and in vivo.

combination_strategies.rationale: Synergize T cell killing by removing tumor intrinsic survival signals (TRAF2/RNF31)
combination_strategies.phenotype: increased sensitivity to T cell killing

open_questions: Whether these heterotypic clusters can be detected in peripheral blood for non-invasive monitoring.
Whether T cells at the tumor margin of 'cold' tumors form stable clusters with APCs to enable isolation of reactive T cells.

session_title: Harnessing the Power of the Immune System: Turning Cold Tumors Hot
talk_title: Myeloid-Mediated Immune Suppression in Prostate Cancer and Bone Metastasis

biomarkers.name: SPP1-high TAMs
biomarkers.finding: Patients with high levels of SPP1 TAMs at baseline responded to zafirlukast + atezolizumab (ORR ~20% in CRPC).

clinical_data.population: CRPC patients with high SPP1 TAMs

MOA_summary: The speaker identifies two distinct myeloid-mediated resistance mechanisms in prostate cancer: SPP1-high TAMs in primary/soft tissue tumors that suppress via the CD73-adenosine pathway, and IL-1beta-high mature neutrophils in bone metastases that suppress via CXCR2-mediated recruitment and IL-1beta signaling.
key_findings: Sipuleucel-T induces T cells that are 'excluded' at the invasive margin, limiting clinical efficacy.
SPP1-high TAMs produce adenosine via CD73; blocking the A2A receptor with zafirlukast combined with anti-PD-1 showed clinical responses in 20% of CRPC patients.
Bone metastases are uniquely enriched for IL-1beta-high mature neutrophils, which are functionally immunosuppressive in vitro and in vivo.
Inhibition of CXCR1/2 or IL-1beta receptor rescued the efficacy of combination checkpoint inhibition in bone metastasis mouse models.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Bone metastasis environments are dominated by neutrophils rather than TAMs, requiring different therapeutic strategies than soft tissue metastases.

combination_strategies.rationale: Block adenosine-mediated suppression to enhance checkpoint blockade
combination_strategies.phenotype: clinical responses in 20% of CRPC patients

combination_strategies.rationale: Prevent migration of suppressive neutrophils into bone metastasis
combination_strategies.phenotype: enhancement of anti-tumor response in mouse model

open_questions: Whether the role of IL-6 contributes to the immunosuppressive phenotype of neutrophils in bone metastases.
Whether epigenetic subtypes (e.g., neuroendocrine vs AR-positive) drive the different myeloid compositions across metastatic sites.
